title: glochidion granulare
intro: Glochidion granulare, also known as the Burmese star-cheese tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Phyllanthaceae. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Glochidion granulare include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and digestive issues, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and improve immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in infusions, decoctions, or as a topical application, and it is also sometimes used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
